chapter_title.ar: باب زَكَاةِ مَالِ الْمَمْلُوكِ وَ الْمُكَاتَبِ وَ الْمَجْنُونِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 24 – Zakāt on the wealth of the owned slaves, and the contracted slaves, and the insane

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/77)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/77)
text: لَيْسَ فِي مَالِ الْمَمْلُوكِ شَيْ‏ءٌ وَ لَوْ كَانَ لَهُ أَلْفُ أَلْفٍ وَ لَوِ احْتَاجَ لَمْ يُعْطَ مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ شَيْئاً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is nothing regarding the wealth of the owned slaves, and even though there may be thousands upon thousand for him, and even if he was in need, he would not be given anything from the Zakāt’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ’Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Zakat is not due on the asset of slaves even if it amounts to a million; but they cannot receive Zakat if they become needy.”’
